title: Okra — four-stage urea + MOP ramp for a 50–100-day harvest window
body: HORDI runs okra on a notably aggressive top-dressing schedule compared to other vegetables: Urea + MOP at basal (50 + 25 kg/ha), 2 weeks (50 + 25), 5 weeks (100 + 50), and 8 weeks (100 + 50) — with TSP only at basal (195 kg/ha). The front-loaded N drives the continuous pod-set window: picking begins at ~50 days and runs every 2 days for up to 25 picks across 50–100 days after planting. OKH1 hybrid can reach 30 t/ha at this rate; OPVs like MI5, MI7, and Haritha yield 10–15 t/ha. The 5-week and 8-week dressings are the high-leverage ones — missing them sheds pod count sharply.
quote: Basal: Urea 50, TSP 195, MOP 25. Top dressing (2 weeks): Urea 50, MOP 25. Top dressing (5 weeks): Urea 100, MOP 50. Top dressing (8 weeks): Urea 100, MOP 50.
